title: Operating System - Deadlock Prevention Avoidance Detection

questions.question:
When a process is rolled back as a result of deadlock the difficulty which arises is



Operating System: ISRO CSE 2009 | Question: 77

Consider a system having \( m \) resources of the same type. These resources are shared by \( 3 \) processes \( A, B, C, \) which have peak time demands of \( 3, 4, 6 \) respectively. The minimum value of \( m \) that ensures that deadlock will never occur is:



Operating System: ISRO CSE 2018 | Question: 21

An aid to determine the deadlock occurrence is



Operating System: ISRO CSE 2020 | Question: 29

Which of the following is not true with respect to deadlock prevention and deadlock avoidance schemes?



Operating System: ISRO
options: In deadlock prevention, the request for resources is always granted if resulting state is safe.
In deadlock avoidance, the request for resources is always granted. if the resulting state is safe.
Deadlock avoidance requires knowledge of resource requirements a priori.
Deadlock prevention is more restrictive than deadlock avoidance.
questions.correct_answer: In deadlock prevention, the request for resources is always granted if resulting state is safe.
